sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.7 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). Is it true that the reduced$C^*$-alge\-bra of a countable group without amenable normal subgroups distinct from$\{ 1\}$is always a simple$C^*$-alge\-bra with unique trace? See (M.\,B.\,Bekka, P.~de~la~Harpe, Expos. Math., 18, no.\,3 (2000), 215--230). Comment of 2009\/: This was proved for linear groups (T.\,Poznansky, ). { P.~de~la~Harpe} Yes, it is true (E.\,Breuillard, M.\...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
